algae_description: Dense, bright yellow-green moss or fine-leafed plant growth carpeting the substrate. Filamentous and feathery in appearance, healthy and vibrant coloration. Some areas appear taller and more established.
substrate_condition: Dark substrate with dense plant coverage; appears well-established with moss or similar plant material throughout. Substrate itself not directly visible due to heavy plant colonization.
concerns: One red/reddish shrimp visible among dense plant growth — excellent sign of successful cycle progression and established conditions. Plant density is robust. Overall conditions appear supportive for the colony.

substrate_condition: clean dark substrate with good distribution
dark substrate with fine grain, clean appearance
algae_description: bright green filamentous/thread algae growing on and around plant stems; appears healthy and actively growing
concerns: Filamentous algae proliferation — moderate growth, not yet blanketing substrate or plants, but visible and spreading. Expected during nitrogen cycle cycling-in phase. Monitor for explosive growth.

substrate_condition: Dark substrate with good detail visibility; appears clean and well-maintained
algae_description: Bright green feathery algae (likely hair algae or similar filamentous growth) covering plants and substrate in the foreground. Dense, healthy growth pattern.
concerns: Moderate to heavy algae bloom — bright green filamentous growth is extensive and visible throughout the foreground

Day 27 of nitrogen cycle: this algae growth is typical during establishment phase when nutrient availability is rising but biofilter maturity is incomplete. Normal but worth monitoring if it accelerates.

algae_description: Dense, feathery bright green filamentous algae (likely hair algae or similar fast-growing variety) covering the foreground. Growth is lush and prominent, indicating nutrient availability during the nitrogen cycle phase.
substrate_condition: Dark substrate with visible texture/pebbles, clean overall with algae growth on planted matter rather than substrate itself
concerns: Significant algae bloom — expected during nitrogen cycle but worth monitoring to prevent overgrowth
Only one shrimp visible in frame; unclear if others are out of view or if colony is smaller than expected at day 27

narrative: A single cherry shrimp — vibrant red coloration — grips a dark plant stem or piece of hardscape in what appears to be a close-up macro shot. The shrimp is active and well-positioned; water is clear. The image is out of focus and tight, making broader tank assessment difficult, but the animal itself appears healthy and mobile.
